title: Fix form for d: a home-anchored deny Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**) DOES hard-block writes into the additional directory's scripts/ (while the rest of the extra root stays auto-approved)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: Write tool blocked: "File is in a directory that is denied by your permission settings."

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/other/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

hypothesis: W1 remediation: in a multi-root workspace, each root needs its own anchored deny. deny Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**) hard-blocks the edit-family inside the additional directory's scripts/ (which the cwd-relative form in d silently missed), while leaving the rest of the extra root writable under acceptEdits.
